alerts.name: SQL Server CPU Usage
alerts.description: SQL Server process CPU utilization is high
alerts.query: SELECT record.value('(./Record/SchedulerMonitorEvent/SystemHealth/ProcessUtilization)[1]', 'int') AS value FROM (SELECT TOP 1 CONVERT(XML, record) AS record FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WHERE ring_buffer_type = N'RING_BUFFER_SCHEDULER_MONITOR' ORDER BY timestamp DESC) AS x

alerts.name: OS CPU Usage
alerts.description: Total operating system CPU utilization is high
alerts.query: SELECT 100 - record.value('(./Record/SchedulerMonitorEvent/SystemHealth/SystemIdle)[1]', 'int') AS value FROM (SELECT TOP 1 CONVERT(XML, record) AS record FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WHERE ring_buffer_type = N'RING_BUFFER_SCHEDULER_MONITOR' ORDER BY timestamp DESC) AS x

alerts.name: Long-Running Query
alerts.description: A query has been running longer than the threshold
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(DATEDIFF(SECOND, start_time, GETDATE())) AS value FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE session_id > 50 AND start_time IS NOT NULL AND status IN ('running','suspended','runnable')

alerts.name: Excessive Recompilations
alerts.description: SQL recompilations per second exceed threshold
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%SQL Statistics%' AND counter_name = 'SQL Re-Compilations/sec'

alerts.name: Batch Requests Anomaly
alerts.description: Batch requests per second deviate significantly from baseline
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%SQL Statistics%' AND counter_name = 'Batch Requests/sec'

alerts.name: I/O Stall Time
alerts.description: Average I/O stall time is high, indicating disk performance issues
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN num_of_reads + num_of_writes > 0 THEN (io_stall) / (num_of_reads + num_of_writes) ELSE 0 END) AS value FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)

alerts.name: Excessive Page Splits
alerts.description: Page splits per second are high, may indicate poor indexing or fill factor
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%Access Methods%' AND counter_name = 'Page Splits/sec'

alerts.name: Wait Time Anomaly
alerts.description: Total wait time is abnormally high compared to typical workload
alerts.query: SELECT SUM(wait_time_ms) AS value FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN ('SLEEP_TASK','LAZYWRITER_SLEEP','SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH','CLR_AUTO_EVENT','CLR_SEMAPHORE','BROKER_EVENTHANDLER','BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR','BROKER_TASK_STOP','BROKER_TO_FLUSH','CHECKPOINT_QUEUE','DBMIRROR_DBM_EVENT','DBMIRRORING_CMD','DIRTY_PAGE_POLL','DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE','FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT','HADR_FILESTREAM_IOMGR_IOCOMPLETION','HADR_WORK_QUEUE','LOGMGR_QUEUE','ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE','REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH','RESOURCE_QUEUE','SERVER_IDLE_CHECK','SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP','SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP','TRACEWRITE','WAIT_XTP_HOST_WAIT','WAITFOR','XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT','XE_TIMER_EVENT')

alerts.name: SQL Server Response Time
alerts.description: SQL Server response time to a probe query is high
alerts.query: DECLARE @t DATETIME = GETDATE(); SELECT 1; SELECT DATEDIFF(MILLISECOND, @t, GETDATE()) AS value

alerts.name: Page Life Expectancy
alerts.description: PLE is low, indicating memory pressure and excessive physical reads
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%' AND counter_name = 'Page life expectancy'

alerts.name: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio
alerts.description: Buffer cache hit ratio is low, too many reads going to disk
alerts.query: SELECT CAST(a.cntr_value AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(b.cntr_value, 0) * 100 AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters a CROSS JOIN sys.dm_os_performance_counters b WHERE a.object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%' AND a.counter_name = 'Buffer cache hit ratio' AND b.object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%' AND b.counter_name = 'Buffer cache hit ratio base'

alerts.name: OS Memory Usage
alerts.description: Operating system physical memory usage is high
alerts.query: SELECT (total_physical_memory_kb - available_physical_memory_kb) * 100.0 / NULLIF(total_physical_memory_kb, 0) AS value FROM sys.dm_os_sys_memory

alerts.name: SQL Server Memory Usage
alerts.description: SQL Server is using a high percentage of its max configured memory
alerts.query: SELECT CAST(physical_memory_in_use_kb AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(committed_target_kb, 0) * 100 AS value FROM sys.dm_os_process_memory

alerts.name: OS Paging (Swapping)
alerts.description: Operating system is actively paging memory to disk
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%Buffer Manager%' AND counter_name = 'Page reads/sec'

alerts.name: Database Space Full
alerts.description: A database data file is running out of space
alerts.query: SELECT TOP 1 CAST(FILEPROPERTY(name, 'SpaceUsed') AS FLOAT) * 100.0 / NULLIF(size, 0) AS value FROM sys.database_files WHERE type = 0 ORDER BY CAST(FILEPROPERTY(name, 'SpaceUsed') AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(size, 0) DESC

alerts.name: Transaction Log Full
alerts.description: A transaction log file is running out of space
alerts.query: SELECT TOP 1 CAST(FILEPROPERTY(name, 'SpaceUsed') AS FLOAT) * 100.0 / NULLIF(size, 0) AS value FROM sys.database_files WHERE type = 1 ORDER BY CAST(FILEPROPERTY(name, 'SpaceUsed') AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(size, 0) DESC

alerts.name: Disk Space Low
alerts.description: A disk volume used by SQL Server has low free space
alerts.query: SELECT MIN(available_bytes / 1048576) AS value FROM sys.dm_os_volume_stats(DB_ID(), 1)

alerts.name: Data File Auto-Growth
alerts.description: A data file has auto-grown, which can cause performance issues
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.fn_xe_file_target_read_file('system_health*.xel', NULL, NULL, NULL) WHERE object_name = 'database_file_size_change' AND CONVERT(XML, event_data).value('(event/data[@name="file_type"]/text)[1]','varchar(10)') = 'Data' AND CONVERT(XML, event_data).value('(event/@timestamp)[1]','datetime2') > DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, SYSUTCDATETIME())

alerts.name: Log File Auto-Growth
alerts.description: A log file has auto-grown, may indicate missing log backups or large transactions
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.fn_xe_file_target_read_file('system_health*.xel', NULL, NULL, NULL) WHERE object_name = 'database_file_size_change' AND CONVERT(XML, event_data).value('(event/data[@name="file_type"]/text)[1]','varchar(10)') = 'Log' AND CONVERT(XML, event_data).value('(event/@timestamp)[1]','datetime2') > DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, SYSUTCDATETIME())

alerts.name: Virtual Log File Count
alerts.description: Excessive VLFs in a transaction log degrade recovery and log operations
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.dm_db_log_info(DB_ID())

alerts.name: Filegroup Space Full
alerts.description: A filegroup is running out of allocated space
alerts.query: SELECT TOP 1 SUM(CAST(FILEPROPERTY(f.name, 'SpaceUsed') AS FLOAT)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(f.size AS FLOAT)), 0) AS value FROM sys.database_files f WHERE f.type = 0 GROUP BY f.data_space_id ORDER BY SUM(CAST(FILEPROPERTY(f.name, 'SpaceUsed') AS FLOAT)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(f.size AS FLOAT)), 0) DESC

alerts.name: Disk Read Latency
alerts.description: Average disk read latency is high
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN num_of_reads > 0 THEN io_stall_read_ms / num_of_reads ELSE 0 END) AS value FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)

alerts.name: Disk Write Latency
alerts.description: Average disk write latency is high
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(CASE WHEN num_of_writes > 0 THEN io_stall_write_ms / num_of_writes ELSE 0 END) AS value FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)

alerts.name: TempDB Space Used
alerts.description: TempDB data file space usage is high
alerts.query: SELECT SUM(CAST(unallocated_extent_page_count AS FLOAT)) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(total_page_count AS FLOAT)), 0) AS value FROM tempdb.sys.dm_db_file_space_usage

alerts.name: TempDB Contention
alerts.description: TempDB allocation page latch contention detected
alerts.query: SELECT SUM(wait_time_ms) / NULLIF(SUM(waiting_requests_count), 0) AS value FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type LIKE 'PAGELATCH%' AND wait_type NOT LIKE '%IO%'

alerts.name: TempDB Auto-Growth
alerts.description: TempDB has auto-grown, indicating potential sizing issue
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.fn_xe_file_target_read_file('system_health*.xel', NULL, NULL, NULL) WHERE object_name = 'database_file_size_change' AND CONVERT(XML, event_data).value('(event/data[@name="database_id"]/value)[1]','int') = 2 AND CONVERT(XML, event_data).value('(event/@timestamp)[1]','datetime2') > DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, SYSUTCDATETIME())

alerts.name: TempDB Files Different Sizes
alerts.description: TempDB data files have different sizes, causing uneven round-robin allocation
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN MAX(size) > 0 THEN (MAX(size) - MIN(size)) * 100.0 / MAX(size) ELSE 0 END AS value FROM tempdb.sys.database_files WHERE type = 0

alerts.name: Version Store Size
alerts.description: TempDB version store is consuming significant space
alerts.query: SELECT SUM(version_store_reserved_page_count) * 8 / 1024 AS value FROM tempdb.sys.dm_db_file_space_usage

alerts.name: Deadlock Detected
alerts.description: A deadlock has occurred
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%Locks%' AND counter_name = 'Number of Deadlocks/sec'

alerts.name: Deadlock Rate
alerts.description: Deadlocks per minute exceeds threshold
alerts.query: SELECT cntr_value AS value FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE object_name LIKE '%Locks%' AND counter_name = 'Number of Deadlocks/sec'

alerts.name: Blocking Process
alerts.description: A session is being blocked for longer than the threshold
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(wait_time / 1000) AS value FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE blocking_session_id > 0

alerts.name: Blocked Session Count
alerts.description: Number of currently blocked sessions exceeds threshold
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE blocking_session_id > 0

alerts.name: Instance Unreachable
alerts.description: Cannot connect to the SQL Server instance
alerts.query: SELECT 1 AS value

alerts.name: Database Unavailable
alerts.description: A user database is not in ONLINE state
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state <> 0

alerts.name: SQL Agent Stopped
alerts.description: SQL Server Agent service is not running
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Agent%' AND status <> 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS value

alerts.name: SQL Agent Job Failed
alerts.description: A SQL Agent job has failed
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j ON j.job_id = h.job_id WHERE h.step_id = 0 AND h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) > DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, GETDATE())

alerts.name: SQL Agent Job Long Running
alerts.description: A SQL Agent job has been running significantly longer than its average duration
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(DATEDIFF(SECOND, ja.start_execution_date, GETDATE())) AS value FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WHERE ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL AND ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity)

alerts.name: SQL Agent Job Completion
alerts.description: A SQL Agent job has completed (informational)
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WHERE h.step_id = 0 AND h.run_status = 1 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) > DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, GETDATE())

alerts.name: Full-Text Search Stopped
alerts.description: SQL Server Full-Text Search service is not running
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN FULLTEXTSERVICEPROPERTY('IsFullTextInstalled') = 1 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Full-Text%' AND status = 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS value

alerts.name: DTC Service Stopped
alerts.description: Distributed Transaction Coordinator service is not running
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%DTC%' AND status <> 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS value

alerts.name: SQL Browser Stopped
alerts.description: SQL Server Browser service is not running (required for named instances)
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Browser%' AND status <> 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS value

alerts.name: SSIS Service Stopped
alerts.description: SQL Server Integration Services is not running
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE '%Integration%' AND status <> 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS value

alerts.name: Full Backup Overdue
alerts.description: A user database has not had a full backup within the threshold period
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(ISNULL(DATEDIFF(HOUR, b.backup_finish_date, GETDATE()), 9999)) AS value FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name = b.database_name AND b.type = 'D' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY value DESC

alerts.name: Differential Backup Overdue
alerts.description: A user database has not had a differential backup within the threshold period
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(ISNULL(DATEDIFF(HOUR, b.backup_finish_date, GETDATE()), 9999)) AS value FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name = b.database_name AND b.type = 'I' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 AND d.recovery_model_desc <> 'SIMPLE' GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY value DESC

alerts.name: Log Backup Overdue
alerts.description: A database in FULL recovery has not had a log backup within the threshold period
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(ISNULL(DATEDIFF(MINUTE, b.backup_finish_date, GETDATE()), 99999)) AS value FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name = b.database_name AND b.type = 'L' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 AND d.recovery_model_desc <> 'SIMPLE' GROUP BY d.name ORDER BY value DESC

alerts.name: Integrity Check Overdue
alerts.description: No DBCC CHECKDB has been run within the threshold period
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(DATEDIFF(HOUR, DBCC_LastKnownGood, GETDATE())) AS value FROM (SELECT d.name, DATABASEPROPERTYEX(d.name, 'LastGoodCheckDbTime') AS DBCC_LastKnownGood FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0) x WHERE DBCC_LastKnownGood IS NOT NULL

alerts.name: AG Failover Detected
alerts.description: An Availability Group failover event has occurred
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_group_states WHERE primary_recovery_health_desc <> 'ONLINE' OR secondary_recovery_health_desc <> 'ONLINE'

alerts.name: AG Replica Not Healthy
alerts.description: An Availability Group replica is not in a healthy state
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states WHERE connected_state_desc = 'DISCONNECTED' OR synchronization_health_desc <> 'HEALTHY'

alerts.name: AG Replication Falling Behind
alerts.description: Availability Group log send queue or redo queue is large
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(ISNULL(log_send_queue_size, 0) + ISNULL(redo_queue_size, 0)) / 1024 AS value FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states WHERE is_local = 0

alerts.name: AG Estimated Data Loss
alerts.description: Estimated data loss for asynchronous AG replicas exceeds threshold
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(DATEDIFF(SECOND, last_hardened_time, GETDATE())) AS value FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states WHERE is_local = 0 AND last_hardened_time IS NOT NULL

alerts.name: AG Redo Queue Size
alerts.description: Redo queue on a secondary replica is large, indicating apply lag
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(ISNULL(redo_queue_size, 0)) / 1024 AS value FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states WHERE is_local = 0

alerts.name: AG Listener Offline
alerts.description: An Availability Group listener is offline
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.availability_group_listeners agl LEFT JOIN sys.dm_tcp_listener_states tls ON agl.listener_id = tls.listener_id WHERE tls.state <> 0 OR tls.listener_id IS NULL

alerts.name: Cluster Failover
alerts.description: A Windows failover cluster event has occurred
alerts.query: SELECT CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsClustered') = 1 AND NodeName <> (SELECT TOP 1 NodeName FROM sys.dm_os_cluster_nodes WHERE is_current_owner = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS value FROM sys.dm_os_cluster_nodes WHERE is_current_owner = 1

alerts.name: Mirroring Status Change
alerts.description: Database mirroring state has changed from normal
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.database_mirroring WHERE mirroring_state IS NOT NULL AND mirroring_state <> 4

alerts.name: Error Log Entry (Sev >= 17)
alerts.description: A high-severity error has been logged in the SQL Server error log
alerts.query: EXEC sp_readerrorlog 0, 1, NULL, NULL, DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, GETDATE()), NULL; -- post-process for severity >= 17

alerts.name: Error Log Fatal Entry
alerts.description: A fatal error (severity >= 20) has been logged
alerts.query: EXEC sp_readerrorlog 0, 1, NULL, NULL, DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, GETDATE()), NULL; -- post-process for severity >= 20

alerts.name: Login Failure Detected
alerts.description: Failed login attempts detected in the error log
alerts.query: EXEC sp_readerrorlog 0, 1, 'Login failed', NULL, DATEADD(MINUTE, -5, GETDATE()), NULL; -- count rows

alerts.name: Configuration Change
alerts.description: A SQL Server configuration option has been changed
alerts.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS value FROM sys.configurations WHERE value <> value_in_use

alerts.name: SQL I/O Error
alerts.description: SQL Server has encountered an I/O error on a database file
alerts.query: SELECT SUM(num_of_bytes_read + num_of_bytes_written) AS value FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL) WHERE io_stall_queued_read_ms > 0 OR io_stall_queued_write_ms > 0

alerts.name: Index Fragmentation
alerts.description: Significant index fragmentation detected on large tables
alerts.query: SELECT MAX(avg_fragmentation_in_percent) AS value FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 'LIMITED') WHERE page_count > 1000 AND avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 10

alerts.name: User Connections High
alerts.description: User connection count approaching the configured maximum
alerts.query: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) * 100.0 / NULLIF(CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('MaxConnections') AS FLOAT), 0) AS value FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE is_user_process = 1
